gameStart.intro:
Perfect. Allow me to set the scene for you...

You and two other adventurers have been tasked with escorting a carriage containing two crates of a precious cargo,
the likes of which cannot afford to be damaged. You're about three days into your journey, with a couple more to go.
As you begin to settle down for the night, you hear a slight rustling in a nearly bush. You stand betweenthe camp and the bush,
watching it warily, as an angry looking goblin jumps out at you, brandishing a rusty knife.

combatIntro2.intro:
The goblin goes down without much fight, but it makes you wonder why it might have
attacked you alone, and whether there are more lurking about.
As you hear a frantic rustling from behind you, you turn around just in time to
witness an abnormally large goblin making off with a crate of your precious cargo.
You try to chase it down, but are intercepted by another goblin,
this one also significantly larger than normal.

goblinChoicePrompt.choice:
After taking out the goblin, you look around frantically, but to no avail.
The goblin that stole one of your crates is nowhere to be seen. If you continue to your
destination without it, you risk not being paid for your efforts, but following the
goblin may result in your untimely death.

goblinChoice1.choice1:
After instructing your companions to watch the carriage, you run off in
the direction of the goblin. After several minutes of running, you find a small camp of
 goblins, seemingly lead by an orc. You don't stand much chance given the current circumstances,
 but your companions may be able to help you. On the other hand, if you can manage
 to get the drop on the orc somehow, you may be able to get this done yourself.

goblinChoice2.choice2:
You continue on to your destination, the town with a cool name that I'll think of later.
You make it there after a couple days, and follow the address you were given to the workshop
 of the prestigious artificer Toralf. He welcomes your arrival, and is willing to forget the issue of the
missing crate. He pays you the agreed 80 gold coins for your efforts, and you leave him to his creations. As you walk back
 out into town, you start looking around for some more work. After a quick glance around
the town square, you notice a tavern, a store advertising various magical and
non-magical trinkets, and a billboard showcasing several different wanted posters.

elder.quest: Please defeat the goblins in the forest.
